description: Integer 0 encoded as UInt8 (40 00) instead of TinyUInt (13). Writers MUST use TinyUInt; readers SHOULD accept the non-minimal form (§12.1: 'Non-minimal integer encoding — SHOULD accept').

String "a" encoded with the generic 0x6F tag (6F 01 61) instead of TinyString (60 61). Writers MUST use TinyString for 1..15 bytes; readers SHOULD accept the generic form (§12.1: 'Non-minimal string encoding — SHOULD accept').
Empty array encoded as an n=0 array leaf (70 00) instead of the EmptyArray singleton (33). Writers MUST emit the singleton (§7.0); readers SHOULD accept the n=0 leaf (§12.1).

Single-element array [1] encoded with offset width w=1 (tag 0x71, two-byte slots) when w=0 would have fit. Writers MUST pick the smallest fitting W; readers SHOULD accept any W that fits (§7, §12.1: 'Non-minimal offset width — SHOULD accept').

Sum index 5 wrapping integer 1, encoded with the generic 0xD0 form (D0 05 14) instead of TinySum (C5 14). Writers MUST use TinySum for indices 0..15 (§9, §12.2 minimal-encoding table); readers SHOULD accept the generic form (§12.1: 'Non-minimal sum index encoding — SHOULD accept').

Generic Sum with index 16 encoded as a 2-byte overlong varuint (90 00) instead of the minimal 1-byte form (10). The varuint is well-formed (terminator present, ≤ 10 bytes) but not minimal; per §6 readers SHOULD accept non-minimal varuints, while writers MUST emit the minimal form.
